summary: The reproduction bundle clones the pinned vulnerable YesWiki revision, seeds an attacker-controlled syndication page, forces the authenticated admin render path, and verifies execution of the downloaded PHP payload from files/.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the syndication mapping flow to write an attacker-controlled .php file into files/ and return the proof string EVOHUNT_PWNED when that file is requested.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the bundled YesWiki snapshot at 5d1c9076ddfab692573782fd5409062cc1b0b986, built the reproduction image, created the malicious syndication page, triggered the administrator-only mapping render, wrote 848bfb67469274bdec9442d5895a171b0f2476c9_shell.php under files/, and printed PROOF_RESPONSE=EVOHUNT_PWNED followed by RCE_PROOF_OK.
verification_method: The host wrapper runs the container-side validation helper, which fails closed unless the exact dropped filename is created and an HTTP request to /files/<sha1>_shell.php?proof=1 returns the exact proof string EVOHUNT_PWNED.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the bundled local Git snapshot yeswiki.bundle on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, builds the reproduction image, and mounts the checkout read-only at /target-repo.
